snp1.12:1.2: a home gives birth to dust.
snp1.12:1.3: Free from a home, free from intimacy:
snp1.12:1.4: Such is the vision of the sage.
snp1.12:2.1: Who, destroying what’s born, wouldn’t plant (again)
snp1.12:2.3: They call him the wandering, solitary sage.
snp1.12:2.4: He, the great seer has seen the state of peace.
snp1.12:3.1: Considering the ground, crushing the seed,
snp1.12:3.3: —truly a sage— seer of the ending of birth,
snp1.12:3.4: abandoning conjecture, he cannot be classified.
snp1.12:4.2: not longing for any one anywhere
snp1.12:4.3: —truly a sage— with no coveting, without greed,
snp1.12:4.4: he does not build, for he has gone beyond.
snp1.12:5.2:
With regard to all things:
unsmeared.
snp1.12:5.3: Abandoning all, in the ending of craving, released:
snp1.12:5.4: The enlightened call him a sage.
snp1.12:6.1: With discernment his strength, well-endowed in habit & practice,
snp1.12:6.3: released from attachments, free from rigidity, free from effluent:
snp1.12:6.4: The enlightened call him a sage.
snp1.12:7.2: uncomplacent, unshaken by praise or blame—
snp1.12:7.3: unstartled, like a lion at sounds,
snp1.12:7.4: uncaught, like the wind in a net,
snp1.12:7.5: unsmeared, like a lotus in water,
snp1.12:7.6: leader of others, by others unled:
snp1.12:7.7: The enlightened call him a sage.
snp1.12:8.1: Who becomes like the pillar at a bathing ford,
snp1.12:8.3: he, without passion, his senses well-centered:
snp1.12:8.4: The enlightened call him a sage.
snp1.12:9.1: Truly poised, straight as a shuttle,
snp1.12:9.4: The enlightened call him a sage.
snp1.12:10.1: Restrained in mind, he does no evil.
snp1.12:10.4: The enlightened call him a sage.
snp1.12:11.1: From the best the middling the leftovers
snp1.12:11.2:
he receives alms.
Sustaining himself on what others give,
snp1.12:11.4: The enlightened call him a sage.
snp1.12:12.1: The wandering sage abstaining from sex,
snp1.12:12.2: in youth bound by no one,
snp1.12:12.3: abstaining from intoxication complacency, totally apart:
snp1.12:12.4: The enlightened call him a sage.
snp1.12:13.1: Knowing the world, seeing the highest goal,
snp1.12:13.3: his chains broken, unattached, without effluent:
snp1.12:13.4: The enlightened call him a sage.
snp1.12:14.1: These two are different, they dwell far apart:
snp1.12:14.2: the householder supporting a wife and the unselfish one, of good practices.
snp1.12:14.3: Slaying other beings, the householder is unrestrained.
snp1.12:14.4: Constantly the sage protects other beings, is controlled.
snp1.12:15.1: Just as the crested, blue-necked peacock,
snp1.12:15.2: when flying, never matches the wild goose in speed,
snp1.12:15.3: even so the householder never keeps up with the monk—
snp1.12:15.4: the sage secluded in the forest, doing jhāna.
